meta.title: Studio Novák · fine-art wedding photographer · Prague
meta.description: Fine-art wedding and portrait photography in Prague, Vienna and beyond. Sofia Novák — quietly cinematic wedding stories.

s1.title: A garden wedding in autumn light
s1.text: A small ceremony in the courtyard, a long table of friends and an evening that drifted from coffee to candles. We followed the light and stayed quiet — the kind of day that's better when nobody poses for the camera.

s2.title: A summer ceremony by the river
s2.text: A weekend in Český Krumlov — vows on the riverbank, dinner under the chestnut trees and dancing until the lanterns went out. Soft, golden and a little wild around the edges.

s3.title: A city-hall wedding, one good dinner
s3.text: An intimate elopement at Vienna town hall, an afternoon walk through Augarten and dinner for twelve at Tian. No timeline, no stress — only a day they'd happily live again.

about.label: About the photographer
about.title: Hello, I'm Sofia
about.p1: I've been photographing for eight years. After assisting in Prague and a year in Berlin, I've run my own studio in Vinohrady since 2018.
about.p2: My work is quiet. I look for the moment just after the planned one — when nobody's performing for the camera, but everything is still happening.
about.p3: I work mostly with natural light, on a Sony A7 IV with 50 mm and 85 mm prime lenses. I edit in Capture One — soft, warm and with a gentle film grain.

c1.title: Couples session
c1.desc: A relaxed 90-minute session for two — in the city, in the countryside or anywhere with a story. Includes 40+ edited photos and an online gallery.

c2.title: Wedding collection
c2.desc: Full-day weddings in Prague, the Czech countryside or as a destination. Coverage from getting ready to the first dance, with an unhurried, story-led approach.

c3.title: Editorial commissions
c3.desc: Brand campaigns, restaurant menus, magazine commissions and creative editorials. Half-day and full-day rates with full commercial usage rights.

j1.title: Why I shoot fewer weddings each year
j1.excerpt: A short note on why I cap my calendar at 14 weddings a year, what that does for the work, and why it really matters to me.

j2.title: A guide to winter elopements in Prague
j2.excerpt: Light, locations and timing — a practical guide for couples planning a small winter wedding in the city.

j3.title: Five questions before you book
j3.excerpt: A short list of things worth asking any wedding photographer before you sign a contract — useful even if you don't end up booking me.

inq.label: Get in touch
inq.title: Tell me your story
inq.text: I read every enquiry myself and reply within 48 hours with a tailored offer and the full details of every collection.

portf.meta_desc: Selected work — weddings, elopements and editorial. Prague, Vienna and beyond.
portf.title: Selected works
portf.lead: A small, careful selection from the last two years. The full archive is shared with prospective clients on request.

jrn.meta_desc: Long-form notes from the studio — featured weddings, planning advice and the things we're thinking about.
jrn.title: Journal
jrn.lead: Featured weddings, both technical and reflective notes, and the questions I get asked most often. New entries roughly every six weeks.
jrn.p1_title: Klára & Tomáš at the farmhouse — a study in afternoon light
jrn.p1_ex: Sixty guests, one renovated farmhouse north of Prague and exactly the kind of cream-linen tablecloth the wind kept playing with. We arrived at noon and didn't put the camera down for nine hours. The full story, technical notes and why the bouquet shed petals with every step she took.
jrn.p2_title: Eight guests, a forest and the kind of quiet that doesn't survive a wedding party
jrn.p2_ex: Eva and Marek's elopement near Karlštejn — why I love days like this even more than the big ones, and why "small" never means "simple".
jrn.p3_title: A timeline that won't ruin your wedding day
jrn.p3_ex: The single biggest gift you can give yourselves: thirty unscheduled minutes between the ceremony and the reception. A practical guide to building a day that actually breathes.
jrn.p4_title: Anna & Jan, a city wedding at Villa Pellé
jrn.p4_ex: Eighty-five guests, three full installations and a bouquet that weighed exactly the right amount. Why I still shoot the first dance on film, even now.
jrn.p5_meta: On the craft · 4 February 2026 · 9 min read
jrn.p5_title: Why I still shoot on film (some of it, some of the time)
jrn.p5_ex: Not out of nostalgia. Not for marketing. A practical answer to a question I get every week: why film really matters, and when I choose digital instead.
jrn.p6_title: "How many photos will we get?" and other things you don't need to worry about
jrn.p6_ex: Six recurring questions, six honest answers — including why the number you actually want isn't 1,500 photos.
jrn.p7_title: Where I travel — and why I don't take every wedding
jrn.p7_ex: A look at my calendar for 2026 and 2027. I work with a maximum of fourteen weddings a year — and here's what that means in practice.
